meaning: n.有钱人,名人,爱打扮的人,花花公子
examples: Some said he was a toff in disguise.
The toff was right. And on that night in the long ago he had been Youth.
The device then stays off for a period, TOFF, that is 32 times the TON delay.
The tribalists badly underestimated Mr Cameron, blindly dismissing him as a jejune, upstart toff.
'Go back to England, you Toff' is the edited version of the message delivered by the driver.
